assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Provides recurring weekend maintenance windows with Saturday 2-6 AM using FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA recurrence pattern

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: Shows complete gcloud commands with --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-end, and --maintenance-window-recurrence parameters for Saturday weekly schedule

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands for quarterly code freeze exclusions using 30-day periods (e.g., March 15 - April 15, June 15 - July 15)

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Includes specific commands for November audit exclusion covering the entire month (November 1-30, 2025)

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Does not mention the 30-day maximum limit for maintenance exclusions or explain how to handle longer freeze periods

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Does not explain the distinction between manual and automatic upgrades or that maintenance windows only affect auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends using Stable channel and explains why it's appropriate for financial services environments requiring maximum stability

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Shows complete gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time parameters for all exclusions

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Does not warn about the risks of falling behind on versions during extended exclusion periods or the potential security implications
